title: 10 best herbal teas for vomiting
intro_desc: Herbal teas for vomiting are infusions made from various plants and spices that can help alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting. They often contain natural ingredients that have anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and calming properties, which can help soothe an upset stomach and reduce the frequency and intensity of vomiting. Examples of herbal teas that can help with vomiting include ginger, chamomile, peppermint, and licorice root tea. For instance, ginger tea is well-known for its ability to alleviate nausea and vomiting, particularly during pregnancy or after surgery. Chamomile tea, on the other hand, can help relax the stomach muscles, reducing the likelihood of vomiting. By using these herbal teas, individuals can find relief from vomiting, improving their quality of life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mugwort tea helps with vomiting because it contains anti-emetic and calming properties that help soothe the digestive system and alleviate feelings of nausea. The herb's volatile oils and flavonoids work together to regulate gut muscle contractions and reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, thereby preventing unnecessary vomiting. Additionally, mugwort tea can help stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, ensuring proper nutrient absorption and promoting overall digestive health. Its soothing warmth and subtle earthy flavor make it an ideal natural remedy for those experiencing stomach discomfort or vomiting.
remedy_properties: Antiemetic: Mugwort tea can help reduce vomiting by containing volatile oils such as camphor and camphene, which are known to have antiemetic properties.
Carminative: The carminative properties of mugwort tea, due to its content of essential oils like cineole and flavonoids, can help alleviate vomiting caused by gastric discomfort.

Antispasmodic: Mugwort tea contains antispasmodic compounds like chamazulene and flavonoids, which can help relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract and reduce vomiting caused by muscle spasms.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of mugwort tea, attributed to its content of phenolic acids and flavonoids, can help soothe inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby reducing vomiting caused by inflammation.
Nervine: Mugwort tea has nervine properties due to its content of essential oils and flavonoids, which can help calm the nervous system, reducing vomiting caused by anxiety or stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the mugwort plant are commonly used in medicinal tea for vomiting due to their antiemetic properties.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the mugwort plant are used in medicinal tea for vomiting as they contain volatile oils that help to soothe the stomach.
Flowers: The flowers of the mugwort plant are used in medicinal tea for vomiting as they have calming effects on the digestive system.
Seeds: The seeds of the mugwort plant are used in medicinal tea for vomiting as they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce stomach irritation.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried mugwort leaves and place them in a tea infuser or loose in a teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the mugwort leaves.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the herbal properties.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to remove the leaves.
Drink the mugwort tea while it is still warm to help alleviate vomiting.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with vomiting because it contains glycyrrhizic acid, a compound that soothes the stomach lining and reduces inflammation. This can help alleviate the symptoms of nausea and vomiting, particularly those caused by digestive issues or stress. Additionally, licorice tea can help replenish lost fluids and electrolytes, making it a gentle and natural way to rehydrate the body. The tea's warm temperature can also help relax the stomach muscles, further reducing the likelihood of vomiting. Overall, licorice tea offers a soothing and natural remedy for vomiting, providing relief and promoting recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic: Licorice tea can help reduce vomiting by blocking the action of neurotransmitters in the brain that trigger nausea and vomiting, thanks to the active constituents glycyrrhizin and glycyrrhetinic acid.

Demulcent: The soothing and inflammation-reducing properties of licorice tea can help protect the stomach lining and reduce irritation, thus alleviating vomiting caused by gastric irritation, due to its mucilage content.
Antispasmodic: Licorice tea can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing spasms and cramps that can lead to vomiting, owing to the presence of various flavonoids and volatile oils.

Adaptogenic: Licorice tea can help the body cope with stress and adapt to physical and emotional challenges, which can help reduce vomiting caused by anxiety or stress-related conditions, due to the active constituent glycyrrhizin.

Expectorant: Licorice tea can help clear mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and coughing, which can help prevent vomiting caused by respiratory issues, thanks to its expectorant properties derived from glycyrrhizin and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice tea plant are used to make medicinal tea for vomiting due to their anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the stomach lining.

Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the licorice tea plant are used in medicinal tea for vomiting because of their ability to stimulate the production of mucus in the gastrointestinal tract, which can protect the stomach lining.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice tea plant are used in medicinal tea for vomiting as they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the stomach.

Seeds: The seeds of the licorice tea plant are used in medicinal tea for vomiting because they can help relieve nausea and promote the production of digestive juices, which can help regulate digestion.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried licorice root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the licorice root.
Sip the tea slowly, as needed, to alleviate vomiting.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tea helps with vomiting because it contains volatile oils and phenol compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, which have been shown to alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting. These compounds work by relaxing the smooth muscle of the gastrointestinal tract, which can help to reduce spasms and contractions that can lead to vomiting. Additionally, ginger tea can help to stimulate the production of digestive juices, which can help to break down food in the stomach and reduce feelings of queasiness. Drinking ginger tea may also help to soothe the stomach and provide a calming effect on the body, which can further help to alleviate symptoms of vomiting.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic: Ginger tea helps reduce vomiting and nausea by blocking the serotonin receptors in the gut, which activate vomiting. The active constituents responsible for this property are 6-gingerol and 6-shogaol.

Gastric motility regulation: Ginger tea aids in regulating gastric motility, alleviating vomiting caused by gastrointestinal issues. The primary compounds responsible for this are 6-gingerol, 8-gingerol, and 10-gingerol.
Prokinetic: Ginger tea promotes gastric emptying, reducing symptoms of vomiting associated with gastroparesis. The active constituents responsible for this property are 6-gingerol and 6-shogaol.

Anti-inflammatory: Ginger tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, which can cause vomiting. The primary compounds responsible for this are 6-gingerol, 8-gingerol, and 10-gingerol, as well as zingerone.

Antioxidant: Ginger tea contains antioxidants that protect the gastrointestinal tract from oxidative stress, reducing the likelihood of vomiting. The active constituents responsible for this property are 6-gingerol, 8-gingerol, and 10-gingerol, as well as shogaols and zingerone.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Ginger rhizomes are widely used to make medicinal tea for vomiting due to their potent anti-emetic properties.
Leaves: Ginger leaves have been used in traditional medicine to treat vomiting, thanks to their soothing and anti-inflammatory effects on the stomach.
Seeds: Ginger seeds are sometimes used in medicinal tea for vomiting, as they contain compounds that help regulate digestion and reduce nausea.
Flowers: Ginger flowers can also be used in tea to alleviate vomiting, as they contain chemicals that help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Peel and chop 1-2 inches of fresh ginger root, then let it steep in boiling water for 10 minutes.
Measure 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil in a small saucepan.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried chamomile flowers to the boiling water.
Steep for 5 minutes, then strain the tea to remove the solids.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with vomiting because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive system and alleviate symptoms of nausea. It is a mild and gentle herbal tea that can help rehydrate the body and provide much-needed electrolytes during bouts of vomiting. White tea is also caffeine-free, making it a gentle choice for those who may be sensitive to caffeine. Its calming effects on the stomach and digestive tract can help reduce the frequency and intensity of vomiting, providing relief and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic: White tea contains anti-emetic properties that help reduce nausea and vomiting, important for vomiting as it helps restore normal digestive function. This is attributed to the presence of tannins and flavonoids.

Antioxidant: White tea has high antioxidant properties that help protect the digestive tract lining from damage, important for vomiting as it can help prevent further irritation and inflammation. This is due to the presence of high levels of catechins.

Anti-inflammatory: White tea possesses anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, important for vomiting as it can help alleviate symptoms of nausea and discomfort. This is attributed to the presence of epigallocatechin-3-gallate (EGCG).

Antibacterial: White tea has antibacterial properties that help eliminate harmful bacteria in the digestive tract, important for vomiting as it can help prevent infections that can cause vomiting. This is due to the presence of polyphenols.

Gastroprotective: White tea has gastroprotective properties that help protect the stomach lining from damage, important for vomiting as it can help prevent further damage and irritation. This is attributed to the presence of caffeine and theaflavins.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, white tea leaves can help soothe the stomach and alleviate nausea.
Buds: High in tannins, white tea buds can help reduce inflammation and alleviate vomiting.
Roots: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, white tea roots can help calm the digestive system and reduce vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and let it cool down to 175-185°F (80-85°C), as boiling water can damage the delicate properties of white tea.
Add 1-2 teaspoons (2-4 grams) of dried herbal white tea leaves (such as Pai Mu Tan or Silver Needle) to a teapot or infuser.
Pour the heated water over the tea leaves and let them steep for 3-5 minutes, allowing the flavors and medicinal properties to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the spent tea leaves.
Sip the herbal white tea slowly to soothe the stomach and help alleviate vomiting. Enjoy up to 3 cups per day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with vomiting because it contains volatile oils, such as anethole, which can help to relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce inflammation. This can help to alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting, making it a gentle and natural remedy for an upset stomach. Additionally, fennel tea has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can help to improve digestion and reduce the likelihood of vomiting. The tea's mild flavor and soothing warmth can also help to calm a queasy stomach, providing quick and effective relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic: Fennel tea can help reduce vomiting by calming the stomach and reducing spasms, thanks to its active constituents, such as anethole and fenchone.
Carminative: Fennel tea can alleviate vomiting caused by gastric discomfort, as it helps to expel gas and relieve bloating, due to its volatile oils.
Antispasmodic: Fennel tea can help soothe the gastrointestinal tract and reduce vomiting caused by muscle spasms, because of its rosmarinic acid and flavonoid content.
Gastroprotective: Fennel tea can protect the stomach lining and reduce vomiting caused by inflammation or irritation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Digestive stimulant: Fennel tea can promote healthy digestion and reduce vomiting caused by indigestion or slow digestion, thanks to its active compounds, such as estragole and limonene.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are commonly used as they contain oils with antispasmodic properties that can help relieve vomiting.
Leaves: The leaves of the fennel plant have calming effects that can help settle the stomach and reduce the urge to vomit.
Rhyzomes: Fennel rhyzomes are used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help soothe the digestive tract and alleviate vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Crush one teaspoon of fennel seeds using a mortar and pestle.
Place the crushed seeds in a tea infuser and put it in a cup.
Pour boiling water over the infuser, ensuring the seeds are fully submerged.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the infuser and enjoy the warm herbal fennel tea.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with vomiting because it contains antispasmodic properties that can help soothe and relax the digestive tract, thereby reducing the likelihood of vomiting. Its calming effects on the stomach can also help alleviate symptoms of nausea and promote overall digestive health. Additionally, lemon balm has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help combat any underlying infections or inflammation that may be contributing to vomiting. Furthermore, its soothing and slightly sedative properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can also exacerbate symptoms of vomiting.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic: Lemon balm tea can help reduce vomiting by calming the stomach and reducing the feeling of nausea, thanks to its active constituents such as rosmarinic acid and citral.

Carminative: Lemon balm tea can help alleviate vomiting caused by gastric discomfort and bloating, as it aids in the expulsion of intestinal gas, due to its essential oils like citronellal and geraniol.
Anxiolytic: Lemon balm tea can help manage vomiting triggered by anxiety and stress, as it has a calming effect on the nervous system, attributed to its rosmarinic acid and eugenol content.
Antispasmodic: Lemon balm tea can help relieve vomiting caused by muscle spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, owing to its antispasmodic properties derived from rosmarinic acid and eugenol.

Antioxidant: Lemon balm tea can help protect the gastrointestinal system from oxidative damage, which may contribute to vomiting, due to its high antioxidant content, including rosmarinic acid, caffeic acid, and ferulic acid.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm tea plant are commonly used in medicinal tea for vomiting due to their antispasmodic and calming properties that can help soothe an upset stomach.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm tea plant are used in medicinal tea for vomiting due to their carminative properties that can help relieve bloating and gas.
Stems: The stems of lemon balm tea plant are used in medicinal tea for vomiting due to their ability to promote sweating and reduce fever, which can help alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves or use 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Rinse the leaves under cold water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Place the leaves in a teapot and pour 8 ounces of boiling water over them.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes to extract the medicinal compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the leaves, and sip it slowly to alleviate vomiting.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano tea helps with vomiting because it contains several volatile oils, such as carvacrol and thymol, which have antispasmodic and calming effects on the gastrointestinal tract. These oils can help to reduce inflammation and muscle spasms in the stomach, thereby alleviating the symptoms of nausea and preventing vomiting. Additionally, oregano tea has been shown to have antibacterial and antiviral properties, which can help to eliminate any underlying infections that may be causing vomiting. The soothing and warming effect of oregano tea can also help to relax the mind and body, providing further relief from vomiting and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic: Oregano tea can help reduce vomiting by containing anti-emetic compounds, such as carvacrol and thymol, that help regulate the digestive system and suppress nausea.

Antispasmodic: The tea's antispasmodic properties, derived from compounds like rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid, help soothe gastrointestinal spasms and alleviate vomiting caused by intestinal cramping.

Antibacterial: Oregano tea's antibacterial components, including carvacrol and thymol, can eliminate harmful bacteria in the digestive tract, which may help reduce vomiting caused by bacterial infections or food poisoning.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of oregano tea, due to compounds such as rosmarinic acid and β-caryophyllene, can help decrease inflammation in the digestive system, reducing vomiting associated with inflammatory conditions.

Antioxidant: Oregano tea's high antioxidant content, primarily from rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids, aids in protecting the gastrointestinal system from oxidative stress, which may help alleviate vomiting caused by oxidative damage or stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the oregano tea plant are commonly used in medicinal teas for vomiting due to their anti-inflammatory and calming properties.
Flowers: The flowers of the oregano tea plant are used in medicinal teas for vomiting because they contain flavonoids that help reduce spasms in the gastrointestinal tract.
Seeds: The seeds of the oregano tea plant are used in medicinal teas for vomiting as they contain thymol, which has antispasmodic properties that can help soothe the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Crush 1 tablespoon of dried oregano leaves in a mortar and pestle for 2 minutes to release their oils.
Place the crushed oregano leaves in a tea infuser and steep in boiling water for 10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser and stir in 1 teaspoon of honey for added flavor and soothing properties.
Let the tea cool down to a drinkable temperature, usually around 5-10 minutes.
Sip the oregano tea slowly, allowing it to coat your throat and soothe your stomach, until finished.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tea helps with vomiting because it contains antispasmodic and carminative properties that can help soothe the stomach and reduce the likelihood of nausea and vomiting. The volatile oils found in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, have been shown to have antiemetic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of vomiting. Additionally, thyme tea can help rehydrate the body and replenish essential minerals lost due to vomiting. Its mild flavor and aroma can also help stimulate the senses and promote relaxation, making it a soothing and effective natural remedy for vomiting.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic: Thyme tea contains anti-emetic properties that help reduce the frequency and intensity of vomiting by inhibiting the vomiting reflex, thanks to the active constituents thymol and carvacrol.
Carminative: Thyme tea has carminative properties that help relieve symptoms of bloating and gas, which can often trigger vomiting, due to its volatile oils, including thymol and carvacrol.

Antispasmodic: Thyme tea has antispasmodic properties that help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing the likelihood of vomiting caused by muscle spasms, as a result of its active constituents, such as thymol, carvacrol, and flavonoids.
Antimicrobial: Thyme tea has antimicrobial properties that help eliminate bacteria and viruses that can cause vomiting, thanks to the active constituents thymol and carvacrol.

Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of vomiting, due to its active constituents, including flavonoids and phenolic acids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in volatile oils, including thymol, which can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea, making them effective in treating vomiting.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain flavonoids that have antispasmodic properties, helping to relax the stomach muscles and alleviate vomiting.
Stems: Thyme stems contain tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, providing relief from vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Measure - Boil 1 cup of water and add 1-2 teaspoons of dried thyme or 2-3 teaspoons of fresh thyme leaves.
Steep - Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes, covered.
Strain - Strain the tea to remove the herbs.
Sweeten (optional) - Add honey or lemon to taste.
Consume - Sip the tea slowly while in a comfortable position to soothe the stomach and help manage vomiting.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamon tea helps with vomiting because it contains a compound called cinnamaldehyde, which has been shown to help alleviate digestive issues such as nausea and vomiting. The soothing and warming properties of cinnamon tea can help to calm an upset stomach and promote healthy digestion. Additionally, cinnamon tea can help to rehydrate the body, which is important after bouts of vomiting. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of cinnamon tea may also help to protect the stomach lining and promote overall gut health. Furthermore, the pleasant aroma of cinnamon tea can help to stimulate the senses and distract from feelings of nausea.
remedy_properties: Anti-emetic: Cinnamon tea can help reduce vomiting by calming the digestive tract and reducing nausea, due to the presence of active constituents such as cinnamaldehyde and eugenol.
Carminative: Cinnamon tea can help alleviate vomiting caused by gastric discomfort, as it aids in the expulsion of intestinal gas and soothes the stomach lining.
Antispasmodic: Cinnamon tea can help relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, thereby reducing the likelihood of vomiting due to spasms or cramps.
Anti-inflammatory: Cinnamon tea can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate vomiting caused by irritation or inflammation.
Antimicrobial: Cinnamon tea can help eliminate harmful bacteria in the digestive tract, which can help reduce vomiting caused by bacterial infections or food poisoning.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, cinnamon roots can help soothe the digestive system and alleviate vomiting.
Bark: The bark of the cinnamon tea plant contains essential oils and tannins that can help reduce nausea and vomiting, while also providing a calming effect on the stomach.
Seeds: High in antioxidants and essential oils, cinnamon seeds can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing the likelihood of vomiting.
Leaves: The leaves of the cinnamon tea plant contain flavonoids and other compounds that can help alleviate vomiting and improve overall digestive health.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 cinnamon stick (3-4 inches long) and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove cinnamon stick and let the tea cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Optionally, add 1 teaspoon of honey for added flavor and sweetness.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe your stomach and help alleviate vomiting.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cardamom tea helps with vomiting because it contains volatile oils, such as limonene and cineole, which can help to soothe the digestive system and alleviate feelings of nausea. The tea also has antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax the stomach muscles and reduce the likelihood of vomiting. Additionally, cardamom tea can help to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can aid in the breakdown and absorption of food, further reducing the likelihood of vomiting. The tea is also caffeine-free, making it a gentle and soothing option for those experiencing vomiting.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Cardamom tea helps to alleviate vomiting by expelling gas from the stomach, thus preventing the buildup of pressure and discomfort that can lead to vomiting. This is due to the presence of volatile oils such as cineole and limonene.

Antiemetic: Cardamom tea contains compounds that help to reduce the likelihood of vomiting by regulating the digestive system and soothing the stomach lining. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils, flavonoids, and tannins.

Anti-inflammatory: Cardamom tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help to alleviate vomiting caused by inflammation or irritation. This is due to the presence of compounds such as cineole, limonene, and alpha-terpinyl acetate.

Antispasmodic: Cardamom tea has antispasmodic properties that help to relax the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, reducing the likelihood of spasms and cramps that can lead to vomiting. This is due to the presence of volatile oils such as cineole and limonene.

Stomachic: Cardamom tea has stomachic properties that help to tone and strengthen the stomach, improving its ability to digest food and reducing the likelihood of vomiting caused by indigestion or other digestive issues. This is due to the presence of compounds such as tannins, flavonoids, and volatile oils.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Cardamom tea made from seeds is known to soothe the stomach and alleviate symptoms of vomiting.
Leaves: The leaves of the cardamom tea plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat nausea and reduce the likelihood of vomiting.
Flowers: Cardamom tea made from the plant's flowers can help relax the stomach and provide relief from vomiting.
Roots: Although not as commonly used, the roots of the cardamom tea plant can be used to make a medicinal tea that can help treat vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Crush 8-10 cardamom pods to release the seeds, then grind the seeds into a fine powder.
In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of the cardamom powder with 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture, covered, for 10-15 minutes to allow the water to absorb the cardamom's properties.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solid residue.
Sip the herbal tea slowly, as needed, to alleviate vomiting and soothe the stomach.
